events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need assess scenario: trench protection, sloping wrong for soil type per tabulated data DOC, in-progress, heavy industrial. Hazards: cave-in, engulfment, struck-by, potential fatality. Severity critical. Decision fail. Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant. Work stage in-progress. Percent complete maybe 50? value not asked 0. confidence 1.0. event_time 0. sound_source empty? We can put "" or "N/A". rate 0. order empty. workable empty. findings: slope angle does not match tabulated data for soil classification; inadequate trench protection; risk of cave-in. actions: stop work, evacuate workers from trench, re-slope or bench per tabulated data / competent person soil classification, install protective system (shielding/shoring) if sloping infeasible, inspect by competent person before re-entry, update JHA, train workers. references: OSHA 1926 Subpart P, specifically 1926.652(a)(1), 1926.652(b), Appendix B. Need ensure JSON valid.

  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "critical",

  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Trench side slopes do not match the angle required by the tabulated data for the identified soil type.",
    "Protective system is non-compliant and creates an imminent cave-in hazard.",
    "Workers in or near the trench are exposed to potential engulfment and struck-by hazards."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop work immediately and evacuate all workers from the trench and its hazard zone.",
    "Have a competent person reclassify the soil and determine the correct slope angle or select an appropriate protective system per the tabulated data.",
    "Re-slope or bench the excavation, or install shoring/trench shielding to meet OSHA Subpart P requirements.",
    "Conduct a competent-person inspection before any re-entry.",
    "Review and update the JHA and brief the crew on the correct trench-protection requirements."
  ],
  "rationale": "An excavation sloped at the wrong angle for the soil type lacks the required protective system and presents an imminent danger of cave-in, which can be fatal. The condition is non-compliant with OSHA 1926 Subpart P, so the work must fail and be corrected before it resumes.",
  "references": [
    "OSHA 1926 Subpart P",
    "29 CFR 1926.652(a)(1)",
    "29 CFR 1926.652(b)",
    "29 CFR 1926 Subpart P Appendix B - Sloping and Benching"
  ]
}
